# .

> Codebase analysis generated by Sourcevision.
> When using this document: group related items together and separate unrelated concerns.

## Project Overview

- **Files**: 99
- **Lines of code**: 5,063
- **Languages**: Swift (36), Markdown (27), Other (17), YAML (5), JSON (4)
- **Zones**: 7 (7 with descriptions)
- **Avg cohesion**: 0.00, **avg coupling**: 0.00 (weighted by file count, 6 zones with ≥5 files)
- **Small zones**: 1 excluded from averages (<5 files)
- **Git**: main @ 01b2566
- **Analyzed**: 2026-05-22T20:35:04.140Z

## Architecture Zones

### App Build Scripts (`app-build-scripts`)

Shell scripts that automate macOS desktop app packaging: icon generation, DMG creation, asset compilation, and network-isolation verification.

- Files: 4
- Cohesion: 0.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/app-build-scripts/context.md`

### App Lifecycle (`app-lifecycle`)

Application entry point, environment configuration, platform services, and the scheduler engine that drives bedtime enforcement at runtime.

- Files: 6
- Cohesion: 0.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/app-lifecycle/context.md`

### Core Domain Tests (`core-domain-tests`)

Comprehensive test suite for GoToBedCore covering scheduling calculation, persistence, DST edge cases, sleep-skip logic, contrast, and validation.

- Files: 10
- Cohesion: 0.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/core-domain-tests/context.md`

### Overlay UI (`overlay-ui`)

App packaging assets co-detected with the overlay window and view components that present the bedtime notification overlay to the user.

- Files: 6
- Cohesion: 0.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/overlay-ui/context.md`

### Project Root and Domain Models (`project-root-models`)

Community detection artifact grouping project configuration files alongside GoToBedCore domain models due to shared absence of import edges.

- Files: 7
- Cohesion: 0.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/project-root-models/context.md`

### Settings UI (`settings-ui`)

All SwiftUI components and utilities composing the menu bar interface, settings window, appearance editor, and schedule editing experience.

- Files: 9
- Cohesion: 0.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/settings-ui/context.md`

### Static Site Assets (`static-site-assets`)

Web presence files for the project's public landing page, including SEO metadata, crawler directives, and AI-consumption hints.

- Files: 5
- Cohesion: 0.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/static-site-assets/context.md`

## Key Dependencies

## Findings

### Anti-patterns

- No zone boundary is machine-enforced between the three production UI zones (settings-ui, overlay-ui, app-lifecycle). Swift module visibility (internal vs. public) is the only available compile-time mechanism to prevent settings-ui from importing overlay-ui types or vice versa, but there is no evidence this is used intentionally as an architectural constraint — meaning cross-UI-zone coupling can accumulate invisibly. [warning]
- The dependency direction app-lifecycle → settings-ui (to present the settings window via SettingsWindowController) combined with a potential settings-ui → project-root-models write path means settings-ui is coupled upward to the composition root and downward to the domain layer simultaneously. A pure UI zone should have exactly one dependency direction: receiving data from the domain and emitting user actions upward — not owning window lifecycle or writing domain state. [critical]
- MenuContent.swift (the status-bar NSMenu) is co-located with settings views, making the app's primary navigation surface a subordinate member of a settings zone. Any future requirement to hide, modify, or gate the status-bar menu requires touching settings-ui rather than the composition root, which will surprise contributors. [warning] (settings-ui)
- Palette.swift is the sole visual-token definition file and lives inside settings-ui. If overlay-ui requires the same color values (e.g. for a dark sleep overlay that matches the app theme), the only options are duplication or an undocumented cross-zone import — both erode zone boundaries silently. Move shared visual tokens to project-root-models or extract a ui-shared zone. [warning] (settings-ui)
- SettingsWindowController.swift manages NSWindow lifecycle (show, hide, close, ordering) from within the UI content zone. Window controllers are composition-root concerns: AppEnvironment must call into settings-ui to present or dismiss the window, inverting the expected dependency direction where app-lifecycle owns all windowing decisions. [warning] (settings-ui)
- sitemap.xml and site.webmanifest are hand-authored with no link to the authoritative version variable in app-build-scripts. Each app release can ship with stale metadata in the static site without any CI signal — the dual-artifact release model requires a shared version source, not two independently maintained version strings. [warning] (static-site-assets)

### Patterns

- AppEnvironment is an undocumented composition root — it is the single point where all inter-service wiring happens, but no comment or type annotation marks it as such, increasing the risk that a future contributor adds service coupling elsewhere. [warning] (app-lifecycle)
- No service protocols exist for SchedulerEngine or OverlayController — AppEnvironment couples concrete types directly, making the composition root difficult to test in isolation. [warning] (app-lifecycle)
- The GoToBed app target has no corresponding test target — SchedulerEngine, OverlayController, AppEnvironment, and their inter-service wiring have zero automated test coverage. [warning] (core-domain-tests)
- GoToBedCore's UI-framework-free constraint is valuable but convention-enforced only — a compile-time boundary check (e.g., a Package.swift platform restriction or a CI lint rule) would prevent accidental framework imports from eroding the core layer. [info]
- No automated enforcement of cross-zone layering exists: the architectural boundary between domain (project-root-models), UI (settings-ui, overlay-ui), and composition (app-lifecycle) is expressed only by file organization, not by Swift module visibility rules or CI checks — this boundary will erode silently as the codebase grows. [warning]
- The overall zone structure follows a clean hub-and-spoke pattern with project-root-models as the hub and settings-ui, overlay-ui, and app-lifecycle as spokes — this is appropriate for a macOS menu bar app and should be preserved as the feature set grows. [info]
- NSApp activation policy is switched inside OverlayController.present() as an undocumented side effect — callers have no way to opt out or observe the transition, creating hidden global state coupling. [warning] (overlay-ui)
- Validation logic is expressed as a Schedule extension rather than an injectable validator, coupling the domain model to its own schema enforcement and preventing alternative validation strategies (e.g., stricter import-time vs. editor-time rules) without modifying the model itself. [info] (project-root-models)

### Relationships

- The full inter-service event chain (Store → SchedulerEngine → AppEnvironment closure → OverlayController) is tested by no automated path; a regression in any link would only surface at runtime. [warning]
- If settings-ui components directly mutate project-root-models types rather than delegating writes through a coordinator in app-lifecycle, that is a leaky abstraction: presentation layer is reaching past its intended boundary into the domain layer. [warning] (settings-ui)
- No build step links static-site-assets content to the Swift source zones — llms.txt and sitemap.xml are manually authored, creating documentation drift risk on every feature release. [warning] (static-site-assets)

### Observations

- Isolated files — no import edges between 4 files, cohesion is unmeasurable (reported as 0) [info] (app-build-scripts)
- Shell scripts produce 0-cohesion in import-graph analysis by definition — the metric is an artifact of the analysis tool and does not indicate a structural problem. [info] (app-build-scripts)
- Isolated files — no import edges between 6 files, cohesion is unmeasurable (reported as 0) [info] (app-lifecycle)
- SchedulerEngine is co-located with the app entry point rather than with testable GoToBedCore logic, which may limit unit test coverage of core scheduling behavior. [info] (app-lifecycle)
- Isolated files — no import edges between 10 files, cohesion is unmeasurable (reported as 0) [info] (core-domain-tests)
- Ten test files provide broad coverage of GoToBedCore concerns; mapping each test file explicitly to a corresponding production module would make coverage gaps visible as the codebase evolves. [info] (core-domain-tests)
- Isolated files — no import edges between 6 files, cohesion is unmeasurable (reported as 0) [info] (overlay-ui)
- Non-Swift packaging assets are merged into the overlay UI zone by community detection, masking the true cohesion of the OverlayController/OverlayView/OverlayWindow triad. [warning] (overlay-ui)
- Isolated files — no import edges between 7 files, cohesion is unmeasurable (reported as 0) [info] (project-root-models)
- Project configuration files (Makefile, LICENSE, .gitignore) are merged with core Swift domain models due to shared absence of import edges, obscuring the models' architectural role. [warning] (project-root-models)
- ... and 7 more

### Suggestions

- Add a Swift Package Manager build-phase script or a CI lint rule that verifies GoToBedCore compiles with no AppKit or SwiftUI imports; this makes the UI-framework-free constraint machine-enforced rather than convention-only and will catch accidental framework imports before they reach main. [warning]
- Add a replaceError or catch operator to each Combine sink in the Store → SchedulerEngine → AppEnvironment event chain so that a single domain error does not permanently cancel the subscription; document the expected error types at each link so recovery behavior is explicit. [critical]
- Introduce a single VERSION file at the repository root consumed by both the Makefile (injected as MARKETING_VERSION into xcodebuild) and a docs-generation script (substituted into sitemap.xml lastmod, site.webmanifest version, and any DMG download URLs in docs/) — one source of truth for both release artifacts. [warning]
- Audit MenuContent.swift for @EnvironmentObject or @Binding reads from domain state; if present, introduce a MenuViewModel owned by app-lifecycle that pre-computes the display string and passes it into MenuContent as a plain value — removing the view's direct domain dependency. [warning] (settings-ui)
- Rename Compat.swift to a per-API extension convention (e.g. NSButton+Compat.swift, NSColor+Compat.swift) so each shim carries its own deployment-target precondition comment and can be deleted independently when the minimum OS version advances past the guarded API. [info] (settings-ui)
- WeekdayPicker.swift has no settings-specific logic by name — audit whether it imports any settings-ui types; if it is dependency-free, move it to a ui-shared zone so it is available to overlay-ui or any future scheduling surface without cross-zone reach-ins. [info] (settings-ui)
- Zone "Settings UI" (settings-ui) has at-risk risk (score: 0.50, cohesion: 0.00, coupling: 0.00) — approaching architectural risk thresholds [info] (settings-ui)
- Add a CI step that extracts the CFBundleShortVersionString from Info.plist (or the VERSION variable from app-build-scripts) and writes it into sitemap.xml lastmod dates and any versioned download links in docs/ — eliminating the manual sync requirement on each release. [warning] (static-site-assets)
- Add a docs/404.html file with site navigation so GitHub Pages serves a branded error page instead of the platform default when a URL is not found. [info] (static-site-assets)
- Zone "Static Site Assets" (static-site-assets) has at-risk risk (score: 0.50, cohesion: 0.00, coupling: 0.00) — approaching architectural risk thresholds [info] (static-site-assets)
- ... and 5 more
